days.title: Arrival in Tokyo
days.subtitle: Landing and first tastes of the city

timeline.title: Arrive at Narita International Airport
timeline.detail: Landing at Narita International Airport Terminal 1 or 2. Proceed through immigration and customs with your group of 6. Collect luggage and meet at designated meeting point. Allow approximately 1-1.5 hours for this process. Have passports ready and ensure everyone has completed landing forms in advance to expedite the process.

timeline.title: Narita Express (N'EX) to Shinjuku Station
timeline.detail: Board the Narita Express train directly from the airport to Shinjuku Station. Purchase a group ticket or individual tickets at the ticket counter. The journey takes approximately 60 minutes and offers comfortable seating with luggage storage. This iconic train experience provides your first taste of Japan's renowned railway system. Enjoy the scenic views of Tokyo as you approach the city. Recommended to sit together as a group to take photos and discuss the trip ahead.
timeline.mapQuery: Narita Express, Shinjuku Station, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Hotel Check-in at Hotel Gracery Shinjuku
timeline.detail: Arrive at Hotel Gracery Shinjuku with your group. Check in at reception with all 6 party members. The hotel is located in the heart of Shinjuku, directly connected to Shinjuku Station East Exit. Request multiple room keys and inquire about luggage storage if rooms aren't ready yet. Ask concierge for local recommendations and free maps of the area. Confirm group dinner plans for the evening and get directions to nearby attractions.

timeline.title: Lunch at Local Tonkatsu Restaurant
timeline.detail: Take your group to a nearby tonkatsu (breaded pork cutlet) restaurant in Shinjuku. This authentic Japanese cuisine is perfect for a group lunch after travel. The portion sizes are generous and the atmosphere is casual and friendly. Order set meals which include rice, miso soup, and pickled vegetables. This meal provides energy for afternoon exploration and introduces everyone to traditional Japanese comfort food in an affordable way.

timeline.title: Rest and Settle into Hotel Rooms
timeline.detail: Return to Hotel Gracery Shinjuku to rest and freshen up after travel. Unpack luggage and prepare for evening activities. Take a quick shower or bath. Charge phones and cameras. Regroup in the hotel lobby or designated meeting point around 17:00 to prepare for evening adventure. Use this time to brief the group on the evening itinerary and answer any immediate questions or concerns about the accommodations.

timeline.title: First Ramen Experience at Shinjuku Ramen Alley
timeline.detail: Visit the famous Shinjuku Ramen Alley (Shinjuku Ramen Yokocho), a narrow alley with over 30 small ramen restaurants. Each shop has counter seating and unique specialty broths. Guide your group to a highly-rated shop with English menus available. Ramen is perfect for groups as it's quick, affordable, and authentically Japanese. Watch skilled chefs prepare noodles in front of you. This is an iconic Tokyo experience and excellent photo opportunity for your group.
timeline.mapQuery: Shinjuku Ramen Alley, Shinjuku, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Shibuya Crossing at Night Photography Tour
timeline.detail: Travel by train from Shinjuku to Shibuya Station (approximately 15 minutes). Experience the world's busiest pedestrian crossing at night when it's brilliantly illuminated by neon signs and massive LED screens. This is prime photography time for your group to capture iconic Tokyo nightlife images. Crowds are massive but this is the quintessential Tokyo experience. Position your group at elevated viewpoints like the Starbucks overlooking the crossing for optimal photos. The electric energy and scale of the crossing cannot be overstated.

timeline.title: Nightlife and Street Food in Shibuya
timeline.detail: Explore Shibuya's vibrant nightlife district with your group after the crossing. Visit Takeshita Street or nearby streets for street food sampling including takoyaki (octopus balls), okonomiyaki (savory pancakes), and other Japanese snacks. Enjoy the electric atmosphere, bright neon signs, and energy of young Tokyo. Browse duty-free shops and fashion boutiques that stay open late. This combination of food, shopping, and photography creates perfect group bonding time on your first night in Tokyo.

timeline.title: Return to Hotel Gracery Shinjuku
timeline.detail: Travel back to Hotel Gracery Shinjuku via train or taxi with your group. The hotel is only minutes from Shinjuku Station making return easy and convenient. Review the day's experiences and check-in with everyone about their comfort and readiness for Day 2. Encourage early rest after a full travel day. Provide any necessary information about breakfast times and Day 2 meeting points. Ensure all group members return safely and are settled for the night.

localTips: Buy Suica/Pasmo IC card at Narita Express station for unlimited train travel — ¥2,000 initial, unlimited rides
Exchange money at Narita Airport before leaving — better rates than city center
Download Google Maps offline for Tokyo — essential for navigation without roaming charges
Convenience stores (7-11, Lawson, FamilyMart) are everywhere and stay open 24 hours
Book Robot Restaurant or teamLab Borderless at least 2-3 days in advance online

days.title: Shibuya & Shinjuku Exploration
days.subtitle: Shopping, neon lights, and group adventures

timeline.title: Hotel Breakfast & Check-out Prep
timeline.detail: Wake up early at Hotel Gracery Shinjuku and enjoy the complimentary breakfast buffet featuring Japanese and Western options. Pack day bags and prepare for an early morning at Shibuya Crossing. Meeting point in hotel lobby at 07:45 for group departure. Ensure all 6 members have comfortable walking shoes and cameras ready.

timeline.title: Shibuya Crossing Rush Hour Experience
timeline.detail: Take JR Yamanote Line from Shinjuku to Shibuya Station (5 minutes). Experience the world's busiest pedestrian crossing during morning rush hour with thousands of people. Perfect for group photography and experiencing authentic Tokyo energy. Stand on the Starbucks overlooking platform for best views. This is a must-see iconic location that defines modern Tokyo culture.

timeline.title: Takeshita Street Shopping Spree
timeline.detail: Walk to Takeshita Street in Harajuku (15-minute walk from Shibuya). This famous pedestrian shopping street features trendy boutiques, quirky fashion stores, and unique Japanese youth culture. Browse through colorful shops selling clothing, accessories, and character merchandise. Perfect for group shopping experience. Allow 2 hours to explore multiple stores and interact with locals.

timeline.title: Lunch at Harajuku Crepes & Casual Dining
timeline.detail: Enjoy lunch at one of Takeshita Street's famous crepe stands or nearby ramen restaurants. Groups of 6 can share multiple crepes and try different flavors. Alternatively, visit a casual ramen shop for authentic Japanese noodles. Budget approximately 1,500-2,500 yen per person. Quick meal allows time to continue shopping or rest before afternoon activities.

timeline.title: Meiji Shrine & Omotesando Shopping District
timeline.detail: Visit the peaceful Meiji Shrine surrounded by forest, a spiritual retreat from busy shopping streets. Explore the serene walking paths and learn about Japanese culture. Afterwards, browse the upscale Omotesando shopping district featuring luxury international and Japanese brands. A perfect balance of cultural experience and modern shopping for your group.

timeline.title: Return to Shinjuku & Dinner Preparation
timeline.detail: Take JR Yamanote Line back to Shinjuku Station from Meiji-Jingumae Station (10 minutes). Rest at Hotel Gracery Shinjuku for 45 minutes. Freshen up and prepare for evening karaoke adventure. Regroup in hotel lobby and plan dinner strategy before hitting the nightlife scene in neon-lit Shinjuku.

timeline.title: Dinner in Shinjuku Entertainment District
timeline.detail: Enjoy dinner at a group-friendly izakaya or casual restaurant in Shinjuku's lively entertainment district. Try yakitori, tempura, or tonkatsu with cold beer or sake. Many restaurants welcome larger groups. Budget 3,000-4,500 yen per person including drinks. This area features neon lights and vibrant atmosphere perfect for photography and group bonding.

timeline.title: Karaoke Night Experience at Shinjuku Venue
timeline.detail: Head to a popular karaoke establishment in Shinjuku like Karaoke Kan or Big Echo. Book a private room for your group of 6. Enjoy 2-3 hours of singing, bonding, and fun. These venues offer extensive English and Japanese song libraries, food, and drinks. A quintessential Tokyo nightlife experience that groups love for team building and creating memorable moments together.
timeline.mapQuery: Karaoke Kan Shinjuku, Shinjuku, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Late Night Neon Walk & Return to Hotel
timeline.detail: After karaoke, take a leisurely walk through Shinjuku's glowing neon streets. Photography opportunities abound with illuminated signs, entertainment venues, and bustling crowds. Visit convenience stores for late-night snacks and souvenirs. Walk or take short taxi ride back to Hotel Gracery Shinjuku. This is the perfect time to capture Tokyo's famous nightlife aesthetic for your travel photography.
timeline.mapQuery: Shinjuku Neon District, Shinjuku, Tokyo, Japan

localTips: Shibuya Crossing is most iconic at night (8-10 PM) with neon reflections — go twice for best photos
Takeshita Street opens at 10 AM but gets mobbed by noon; arrive early for better browsing
Book karaoke bars in advance on Tabelog or Hot Pepper; walk-in waits can be 1-2 hours on weekends
Yodobashi Camera and Don Quijote in Shinjuku have massive selections and English-speaking staff
Most clothing shops in Shibuya only stock XS-L sizes; check before entering if shopping for larger sizes

days.title: Asakusa & Traditional Tokyo
days.subtitle: Temples, street food, and cultural immersion
localTips: Arrive at Senso-ji Temple before 7 AM to avoid crowds and catch morning prayers and incense rituals
Nakamise street has 89 souvenir shops — budget at least 2 hours for browsing; bring cash for small vendors
Sumida River cruises depart every 30 minutes from Asakusa Pier; book online 1 day ahead for group discounts
Tsukiji Outer Market is best visited before 11 AM for fresh sashimi and breakfast sets at local spots
The Ginza Line from Asakusa is the oldest Tokyo Metro line — take it to experience vintage train cars

days.title: Harajuku, Omotesando & Meiji Shrine
days.subtitle: Fashion, art, and spiritual spaces

timeline.title: Hotel Breakfast & Check-out Preparation
timeline.detail: Enjoy buffet breakfast at Hotel Gracery Shinjuku featuring Japanese and Western options. Settle any bills and prepare luggage for continued stay. Meet the group in the lobby to confirm Day 4 itinerary and coordinate transport to Harajuku area.

timeline.title: Train to Harajuku Station
timeline.detail: Take the JR Yamanote Line from Shinjuku Station to Harajuku Station (approximately 7 minutes). This is an iconic Tokyo train line offering excellent views. Purchase group IC cards if needed for seamless travel throughout the day.

timeline.title: Meiji Shrine Forest Walk & Spiritual Experience
timeline.detail: Explore the serene Meiji Shrine dedicated to Emperor Meiji, located within a pristine forest. Walk through the peaceful torii gates, observe traditional rituals, and enjoy the tranquility of the woodland path. Excellent photography opportunities with natural light filtering through ancient trees.

timeline.title: Yoyogi Park Group Picnic & Photography
timeline.detail: Adjacent to Meiji Shrine, enjoy a group picnic in expansive Yoyogi Park with pre-arranged Japanese bento boxes and refreshments. Capture candid group photos among seasonal scenery, cherry blossoms (spring), or autumn foliage. Space for team bonding and leisurely strolls through the park.

timeline.title: Omotesando Luxury Shopping & Fashion District
timeline.detail: Stroll down Omotesando Avenue, Tokyo's premium shopping destination featuring flagship stores of Louis Vuitton, Gucci, Prada, and Japanese designers. Architecture is stunning with modern and traditional design elements blended throughout. Perfect for photography enthusiasts capturing fashion and urban aesthetics.

timeline.title: Takeshita Street & Harajuku Youth Culture
timeline.detail: Experience the vibrant youth culture and fashion energy of Takeshita Street, Japan's busiest pedestrian shopping street. Browse trendy boutiques, crepe stands, and quirky fashion shops. Great for group photos amid colorful storefronts and experiencing authentic Harajuku atmosphere.

timeline.title: Afternoon Crepes & Cafe Break
timeline.detail: Stop at a famous crepe shop on Takeshita Street for sweet and savory crepe options. Enjoy refreshments in a casual setting while discussing favorite finds from shopping. Many cafes offer seating areas perfect for the group to relax and recharge.
timeline.mapQuery: Takeshita Street Crepe Shops, Harajuku, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Train Return & Dinner in Shinjuku
timeline.detail: Take JR Yamanote Line back to Shinjuku Station from Harajuku (7 minutes). Head to a group-friendly izakaya or restaurant in Shinjuku for dinner. Popular nightlife district with diverse cuisine options and lively atmosphere perfect for company bonding.

timeline.title: Dinner at Local Izakaya or Restaurant
timeline.detail: Enjoy group dinner featuring Japanese cuisine such as yakitori, tempura, ramen, or sushi. Izakayas offer casual, social dining perfect for team bonding with sake or beer selections. Allow 90 minutes for relaxed meal and conversation with colleagues.

timeline.title: Shinjuku Nightlife & Entertainment
timeline.detail: Explore Shinjuku's vibrant nightlife options including karaoke bars, clubs, or lounges. Popular for company outings with group karaoke providing entertainment and team bonding. East Shinjuku has neon-lit streets perfect for photography of Tokyo's electric nightlife atmosphere.

timeline.title: Return to Hotel & Evening Wind-down
timeline.detail: Head back to Hotel Gracery Shinjuku (5-10 minute walk or short taxi ride depending on activity location). Review the day's photos and plan tomorrow's activities. Rest and prepare for Day 5 of the Tokyo adventure.

localTips: Meiji Shrine opens at sunrise; visit before 8 AM for peaceful forest walks and to see morning rituals
Omotesando is Tokyo's Champs-Élysées — best for window shopping and coffee at high-end cafés rather than buying
Yoyogi Park is perfect for company picnics; rent a lawn space or grab benches near the pond for team photos
The Crepe shops near Harajuku Station are tourist traps — better crepes at smaller vendors on side streets
Takeshita Street (parallel to main Harajuku) has vintage and alternative fashion at lower prices than tourist spots

days.title: Akihabara & Tech Culture Day
days.subtitle: Gaming, anime, electronics, and nightlife

timeline.title: Breakfast at Hotel Gracery
timeline.detail: Enjoy a full Japanese and Western breakfast buffet at the hotel restaurant before heading out for the day. The buffet includes fresh fruits, pastries, rice, miso soup, grilled fish, and local specialties. Refuel the group with coffee and tea for a full day of tech and gaming activities ahead.

timeline.title: Train to Akihabara Station
timeline.detail: Take the JR Yamanote Line or Chuo Line from Shinjuku Station directly to Akihabara Station, approximately 15 minutes. Purchase group IC cards (Suica/Pasmo) for seamless travel. Akihabara is Tokyo's famous electric town and anime/gaming district, perfect for the group's interests. Station has clear signage and English support.

timeline.title: Akihabara Electric Town & Shopping
timeline.detail: Explore the bustling electronics and anime shops of Akihabara's main streets. Browse multi-story electronics retailers like Yodobashi Camera, Akky, and countless anime merchandise shops. The group can split up to photograph iconic neon signs, vintage arcade machines, and unique tech gadgets. Perfect for group photos with vibrant street scenery and electric atmosphere.
timeline.mapQuery: Akihabara Electric Town, Chiyoda Ward, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Lunch at Akihabara Ramen or Tonkatsu
timeline.detail: Enjoy group lunch at a popular local restaurant in Akihabara, such as a ramen shop or tonkatsu establishment. Many restaurants accommodate larger groups and offer authentic Japanese cuisine. Estimated cost is 1,000-2,000 JPY per person. Try local favorites like miso ramen, tsukemen, or crispy pork cutlets with fresh vegetables.

timeline.title: Pokemon Center Visit
timeline.detail: Visit the official Pokemon Center Mega Tokyo located in Akihabara, featuring exclusive merchandise, collectibles, and themed items. The group can browse multiple floors of Pokemon-themed products, take photos with life-sized character displays, and purchase souvenirs. This is a must-see for anime enthusiasts and provides excellent photo opportunities for the team.
timeline.mapQuery: Pokemon Center Mega Tokyo, Akihabara, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Gaming Arcades & Taito Station
timeline.detail: Experience Japan's famous gaming culture at Taito Station or Round1 arcade in Akihabara, where six friends can compete in rhythm games, racing simulators, and classic arcade titles. These massive arcades span multiple floors with hundreds of games. Budget 1,000-3,000 JPY per person depending on game selection. Great team-building activity with group competition.

timeline.title: teamLab Borderless Digital Art Experience
timeline.detail: Head to teamLab Borderless, a cutting-edge digital art museum featuring immersive, interactive installations using projection, light, and technology. The experience spans multiple rooms with seasonal artwork and collaborative displays. Advanced booking is strongly recommended. Allow 2-3 hours for full exploration. Perfect for photography enthusiasts as the group will encounter stunning visual moments throughout the space.

timeline.title: Dinner at Shibuya or Return to Shinjuku
timeline.detail: After teamLab, head to nearby Shibuya or return to Shinjuku for group dinner. Options include izakayas, yakitori restaurants, or upscale dining depending on budget and preferences. Shibuya offers vibrant nightlife atmosphere while Shinjuku provides convenience near your hotel. Budget 3,000-5,000 JPY per person for quality meal with drinks.

timeline.title: Nightlife & Entertainment District
timeline.detail: Explore Shinjuku's vibrant nightlife district including karaoke bars, izakayas, and entertainment venues perfect for groups of six. Visit Omoide Yokocho (Memory Lane) for traditional atmosphere or Memory Lane karaoke for team bonding. The group can enjoy drinks, karaoke, and socialize with colleagues. Shinjuku stays lively until late with plenty of options.

timeline.title: Return to Hotel Gracery & Rest
timeline.detail: Return to Hotel Gracery Shinjuku, which is conveniently located within walking distance of Shinjuku Station's nightlife areas. Check room status and prepare for Day 6 of the trip. The hotel staff can assist with any needs. End the evening winding down in your rooms with snacks from convenience stores if desired.

localTips: teamLab Borderless sells out 1-2 weeks in advance; book tickets immediately at teamlab.art/borderless for your group
Gaming arcades charge per game (¥100-500); popular games like Mario Kart and Time Crisis require ¥500+ deposits
Akihabara's maid cafés require reservation and have steep prices (¥3,000+ per person) — book in advance if interested
The Pokemon Center fills with school groups after 3 PM; visit before noon for easier browsing and merchandise selection
Electronics shops (Bic Camera, Yodobashi) have duty-free purchases for tourists — bring passport and ask for TAX-FREE

days.title: Ginza, Tsukishima & Fine Dining
days.subtitle: Luxury shopping and group dinner experiences

timeline.title: Hotel Breakfast & Check-out Preparation
timeline.detail: Enjoy buffet breakfast at Hotel Gracery Shinjuku including Japanese and Western options. Pack light bags for the day's shopping adventure. Arrange luggage storage at hotel concierge for evening access before group dinner. Confirm group has transportation passes and meeting points.

timeline.title: Train to Ginza Station
timeline.detail: Take the JR Yamanote Line or Tokyo Metro Marunouchi Line from Shinjuku Station to Ginza Station. Journey takes approximately 15-20 minutes. Ensure all 6 group members board together. Ginza is Tokyo's most prestigious luxury shopping district with upscale boutiques and department stores.

timeline.title: Ginza Luxury Shopping District Exploration
timeline.detail: Explore Ginza's world-famous shopping streets including Chuo-dori and surrounding areas. Visit flagship stores of luxury brands like Louis Vuitton, Chanel, Gucci, and Japanese designers. Browse department stores like Mitsukoshi and Ginza Six. Ideal for high-end jewelry, fashion, cosmetics, and electronics. Photography-friendly architecture and window displays throughout district.

timeline.title: Casual Lunch Break in Ginza
timeline.detail: Stop at one of Ginza's mid-range restaurants or food courts for lunch. Options include sushi, ramen, tempura, or tonkatsu. Keep meal under 90 minutes to maintain group schedule. Many establishments can accommodate groups of 6 with advance notice or walk-ins during off-peak hours.

timeline.title: Train to Tsukishima
timeline.detail: Take Tokyo Metro Oedo Line or Yurakucho Line from Ginza Station to Tsukishima Station. Journey approximately 10 minutes. Tsukishima is famous for monjayaki, a savory pancake dish unique to this neighborhood. The street has dozens of monjayaki restaurants and local charm with traditional Tokyo atmosphere.

timeline.title: Tsukishima Monjayaki Street Walking Tour
timeline.detail: Stroll through Tsukishima's famous monjayaki alley with narrow streets and traditional shops. Observe local food vendors and street food stalls. Take photographs of traditional storefronts and atmospheric backstreets. Learn about monjayaki history and preparation methods. Group can sample street snacks and interact with local shopkeepers.

timeline.title: Return to Hotel & Freshen Up
timeline.detail: Take Tokyo Metro back to Shinjuku Station and return to Hotel Gracery Shinjuku. Group members should freshen up, change into formal dinner attire, and regroup in lobby by 18:30. Pick up luggage from concierge storage. Confirm all reservations and transportation to Michelin-recommended restaurant.

timeline.title: Travel to Michelin-Recommended Restaurant
timeline.detail: Depart Hotel Gracery Shinjuku as group of 6 toward reserved Michelin-recommended restaurant. Recommend taxi or group car service for convenience with formal dinner attire. Journey time varies by restaurant location, typically 15-30 minutes depending on selected venue. Ensure punctuality for dinner reservation.

timeline.title: Group Fine Dining Experience
timeline.detail: Enjoy Michelin-recommended multi-course dinner with entire group of 6. Experience authentic Japanese kaiseki, French-Japanese fusion, or contemporary fine dining. Courses typically include appetizers, sashimi, main proteins, seasonal vegetables, rice or noodles, soup, and dessert. Duration approximately 2-3 hours. Perfect bonding experience for company team.

timeline.title: Evening Return & Night Relaxation
timeline.detail: Return to Hotel Gracery Shinjuku by taxi or car service after dinner concludes. Group members can visit hotel bar for post-dinner drinks, enjoy nightcap at Shinjuku nightlife venues nearby, or retire to rooms for rest. Day 7 checkout planning can occur if needed. Wind down after full day of luxury experiences.

localTips: Ginza is expensive but best visited on weekdays when it's less crowded; most shops open at 10 AM and close at 8 PM
Tsukishima's monjayaki is Tokyo's comfort food — book a table at a traditional shop for ¥2,000-3,000 per person
Michelin-starred restaurants in Ginza require 2-4 week advance bookings; use Tabelog or Gurunavi (English available)
Luxury brands (Hermès, LV, Gucci) have lower prices in Tokyo than Europe due to VAT refunds — bring passport for tax-free
Sony Plaza and Apple Store in Ginza are worth visiting for design inspiration; take photos but respect photography rules

days.title: Departure & Last-Minute Highlights
days.subtitle: Final shopping, team lunch, and goodbye to Tokyo
localTips: Arrive at Narita Airport 3 hours before international flight; JAL and ANA flights to Malaysia depart Terminal 1
Airport duty-free shops are more expensive than city center — only buy if you forgot gifts; better deals at depachika (basement food halls)
Narita Express (N'EX) returns to central Tokyo until 9 PM; plan checkout and airport transfer by 6 PM
Buy omiyage (souvenirs) from depachika in major stations before airport — Kit Kat, sake, dried seaweed are popular choices
Mobile phones and SIM cards work up until your flight; use IC cards for all transport to Narita — no need for separate tickets

hotels.name: Hotel Gracery Shinjuku
hotels.note: 7-min walk from Shinjuku Station East Exit, modern high-rise with city views and onsen bath, perfect for groups

budget.note: MAS/ANA ¥40,000-45,000 per person (~RM 1,200 each), economy class, includes 23kg baggage
¥2,000 per card with ¥1,500 usable balance, unlimited subway/train access during stay
¥3,200 per person (~RM 96), immersive digital art museum, must book 1-2 weeks ahead
¥600-2,000 per meal per person, includes ramen (¥900), tempura (¥1,500), sushi (¥2,000), convenience stores (¥500)

budget.item: Karaoke session (6 pax, 2 hours in Shinjuku)
budget.note: ¥1,000 per person per hour (~RM 30), includes 1 drink, private room booking mandatory
¥1,000-2,000 per person for 2-3 hours (Mario Kart, Time Crisis, UFO catchers), 6 pax total
Budget RM 300 per person for omiyage, gifts, duty-free, Shibuya/Harajuku shopping
¥1,080 per person (~RM 32.40), 40-minute scenic cruise, group discounts available online

budget.item: Tsukiji Outer Market & Michelin Dinner (6 pax)
budget.note: Lunch ¥2,000, dinner at Michelin ¥8,000-10,000 per person (~RM 240-300 total)

urgent.label: Book teamLab Borderless tickets
urgent.note: Immersive digital art museum sells out 1-2 weeks in advance for groups; book immediately for all 6 pax on specific time slots

urgent.label: Reserve Michelin-starred restaurant group dinner
urgent.note: High-end Tokyo restaurants (Ginza sushi, kaiseki) require 2-4 week advance bookings; use Tabelog or contact via email

urgent.label: Book karaoke private room in Shinjuku
urgent.note: Popular spots have long waits on weekends; reserve 2-3 days ahead on Hot Pepper or Tabelog for 6-person group

urgent.label: Pre-book hotel rooms (1 extra room for 6 pax)
urgent.note: Hotel Gracery Shinjuku offers group rates; book 3 rooms (2 beds each) on Booking.com or hotel website directly

urgent.label: Check flight schedules and book return flights
urgent.note: MAS and ANA operate daily KUL-NRT routes; Narita Express (N'EX) returns operate until 9 PM, plan departure accordingly

urgent.label: Reserve Pokemon Center Takeshita Street visit time
urgent.note: Not mandatory but recommended to visit before 1 PM to avoid crowds; merchandise restocks are 10-11 AM

urgent.label: Purchase duty-free tax refund forms
urgent.note: Stores in Ginza, Shinjuku, and Harajuku offer tax-free shopping (8% refund) — bring passport and ask for 'tax-free' stickers

urgent.label: Book company group lunch/dinner in Shibuya or Roppongi
urgent.note: Robata grill or izakaya group reservations (20+ pax) need 1-2 week notice; suggest Torikizoku or Kushikatsu Daruma
